parsed_review.summary: CP00-333 is a descriptor-only intake-core pack closing the RP10.P06 permission phase (M06-M10) and opening the RP10.P07 failure foundation (M00-M03), 150 units, risk C, range RP10.P06.M06.S04-RP10.P07.M03.S14. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob only) confirms the pack-level claim is supported: identity/range/risk/unit-count and all deliverable, phase, micro-phase, and micro-title distributions match across prompt, review-baseline, manifest, registry binding (INTAKE_CORE_CP333_PACK_BINDING), and INTAKE_CORE_CP333_REQUIREMENTS. All 7 required public exports exist (service.js create*CaseSet/Descriptor; validators.js validate*Coverage/Descriptor + Hermes/Claude/Closeout packets) and are re-exported via index.js. freezeCp333Result and INTAKE_CORE_CP333_ROW_EXTRAS stamp comprehensive descriptor-only/no-runtime/no-write/no-leak attestations with every sensitive flag false (no runtime permission eval, no audit write, no rollback/retry/lock runtime, no cross-tenant access, no permission/audit/blocked-claim leaks, no Hermes runtime receipt, no real data). Validators enforce these per-row and assert authority boundary claude_is_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false; the validation script deep-equals the contract projection against the code-generated descriptor (drift-proof) and tests (model.test.js:879-931) reassert coverage, descriptor-only rows, boundary, and CP00-334/RP10.P07.M03.S15 handoff. Honesty checks pass: manifest and command-evidence both report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review/pending_final_closeout — the production_ready gate is NOT weakened for descriptor-only work, and production_ready_flag is descriptor-verified scoped. Recorded commands all exit 0 (incl. git diff --check, H10 hermes gate, full validate/test suites). Review-evidence pipeline is healthy per CP178+ hardening: readiness auth-preflight returned valid, non-empty (1455B) JSON with no auth failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output; review-request pins claude-opus-4-8, read_only=true, tools Read,Grep,Glob, json output+schema; validation-report shows forbidden_tools_present=false and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[]. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt naming, never product runtime (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; createIntakeCoreCp333HermesEvidencePacket only validates and returns a frozen object). Claude stays a read-only independent reviewer, not final approver; human final approval for enterprise-trust remains required and is correctly encoded. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one cosmetic P3 labeling nit in the review tooling's changed-file-scope.json (non-blocking). Final closeout remains gated on human approval and the post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json), which are expected to be absent at this review step.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own untracked artifact dir as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-333/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). The cp00-333/ directory is actually this pack's own artifact directory (it holds manifest.json and command-evidence.json that the pack will commit), so labeling it 'unrelated' is imprecise. This has zero impact on scope integrity: active_pack_implementation_refs correctly lists the 8 tracked source/contract/test/script files, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is [], and the manifest's own worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:6017) does NOT include these two paths. Purely a cosmetic inconsistency in the review tooling's scope report, not a defect in the pack deliverables.
findings.recommendation: Optionally refine the scope-report generator so a pack's own untracked artifact directory (docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/) is classified as a pack artifact rather than echoed under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. No change required for this pack's closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic note; does not affect verdict, scope correctness, or any production_ready/no-write/authority-boundary claim.
